moves.note: Add quality tech, stay within limits.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate their risk.
Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital.
Add to QQQI to increase income focus and build a cash buffer.
Add to CRWD to maintain exposure to quality AI/Software.
Buy XLK to add broad tech index exposure, diversifying away from concentration.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This mode targets approximately 1.5% monthly growth. It focuses on preserving capital by cutting leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration, accepting lower potential upside for significantly lower drawdown risk.

plan: Trim SOXL to 3% to reduce leverage risk while retaining minimal exposure.
Trim SPY to 25% to address concentration and free up capital.
Slightly trim QQQ to reallocate capital.
Add to SMH to increase semiconductor exposure as it shows strength.
Buy DDOG, a top scanner setup, for its risk/reward.
Buy XLK to add broad tech index exposure, balancing the portfolio.
balanced.read: This mode targets approximately 2.5% monthly growth. It balances risk reduction with growth by trimming leverage and concentration while adding quality names and broad index exposure.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for higher-growth areas.
Trim QQQI to 5% to shift focus from income to growth potential.
Increase SMH exposure to 15% to capitalize on semiconductor strength.
Increase CRWD exposure to 15% to double down on high-conviction AI/Software.
Add to SOXL (leveraged ETF) to 10% to increase aggressive tech exposure.
Buy TECL and DDOG to add high-scoring, leveraged, and high risk/reward setups.
aggressive.read: This mode targets approximately 4% monthly growth but carries significantly higher risk, including larger drawdowns and amplified losses due to leverage and concentration. Leveraged ETFs also have daily decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

moves.note: Add broad tech index for yield.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate volatility and risk.
Sell high-beta single names (CRWD) and sector concentration (SMH) to reduce risk.
Add significantly to SPY to increase stability and capture broad market distributions.
Add significantly to QQQI to maximize income generation.
Buy XLK to add another broad index ETF for diversified income potential.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and steady monthly income over growth.
income.read: This mode targets approximately 1% monthly growth with very low drawdown. It focuses on generating steady income through broad index and income-focused ETFs, sacrificing growth potential for stability and reliable distributions.
